---
title: "Assignments"
---

Each week has a short set of readings and an AI tutor assignment. The AI tutor assignment is completed on the [course AI tutor site](#) — link and instructions TBD.

---

## Week 1 (Mar 24–26): The Basics of Economics

**Readings**

- Reading TBD
- Reading TBD

**AI Tutor Assignment**

Complete the Week 1 module on the course AI tutor site by **Sunday, Mar 29 at 11:59pm**.

---

## Week 2 (Mar 31–Apr 2): Causation & Experiments

**Readings**

- Reading TBD
- Reading TBD

**AI Tutor Assignment**

Complete the Week 2 module on the course AI tutor site by **Sunday, Apr 5 at 11:59pm**.

---

## Week 3 (Apr 7–9): Education & Discrimination

**Readings**

- Reading TBD
- Reading TBD

**AI Tutor Assignment**

Complete the Week 3 module on the course AI tutor site by **Sunday, Apr 12 at 11:59pm**.

---

## Week 4 (Apr 14–16): Scaling & Partnerships

**Readings**

- Reading TBD
- Reading TBD

**AI Tutor Assignment**

Complete the Week 4 module on the course AI tutor site by **Sunday, Apr 19 at 11:59pm**.

---

## Week 5 (Apr 21–23): Midterm & Incentives

**This Week**

- Tuesday (Apr 21): **Midterm in class**
- Thursday (Apr 23): Complete the materials below before class.

### Class Prep: Incentives

- [Podcast: Why goals, incentives, and choice architecture affect the decisions people make](https://www.hiddenbrain.org/podcast/success-2-0-getting-what-you-want/)
- [E4E Video: Behavioral economics and why people do not always act like the textbook model](https://econ4everyone.uchicago.edu/video/behavioral-economics)

### Optional Readings

- [Optional/Advanced Paper: Evidence on whether paying students for performance improves outcomes](https://www.nber.org/papers/w15898)

### AI Assignment

*No AI tutor assignment this week.*

---

## Week 6 (Apr 28–30): Markets & Equity

### Class Prep: Markets and Their Failures

- [Video: What it really takes to make a chicken sandwich from scratch, and what that shows about production and specialization](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=URvWSsAgtJE)
- [E4E Video: Specialization, trade, and why markets can increase efficiency](https://econ4everyone.uchicago.edu/video/specialization-and-efficiency)
- [E4E Video: Externalities and when markets fail to account for social costs and benefits](https://econ4everyone.uchicago.edu/video/externalities/)
- [Article: Why cuts to university research funding can create spillovers beyond higher education](https://www.npr.org/2025/05/28/1253247256/higher-education-federal-funding-universities-science-research)

### Class Prep: Equity vs. Efficiency

- [E4E Video: The basic tradeoff between equity and efficiency in public policy](https://econ4everyone.uchicago.edu/video/equity-vs-efficiency)
- [E4E Video: A second look at equity vs. efficiency and the costs of redistribution](https://econ4everyone.uchicago.edu/video/equity-vs-efficiency-2/)

### Optional Readings

- [Optional/Advanced Book Chapter: Arthur Okun on redistribution and the efficiency costs of pursuing greater equality](https://www.brookings.edu/books/equality-and-efficiency-the-big-tradeoff/)

### AI Assignment

Complete the Week 6 module on the course AI tutor site by **Sunday, May 3 at 11:59pm**.

---

## Week 7 (May 5–7): Uncertainty & Quasi-Experiments

### Class Prep: Statistical Uncertainty

- [Podcast transcript: Why polling and prediction are hard, and what uncertainty means in practice](https://www.npr.org/transcripts/463237871)
- [Video: A visual introduction to statistical significance, sampling variation, and uncertainty](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kLmzxmRcUTo)
- [Article: Why polls can be wrong even when they are conducted carefully](https://www.nytimes.com/2014/07/04/upshot/why-polls-can-sometimes-get-things-so-wrong.html)

### Class Prep: Quasi-Experiments

- [Podcast: How economists use natural experiments to learn about cause and effect](https://freakonomics.com/podcast/you-make-me-feel-like-a-natural-experiment/)
- [Article: Nobel Prize overview of natural experiments in economics](https://www.nobelprize.org/uploads/2021/10/popular-economicsciencesprize2021-3.pdf)

### Optional Readings

- [Optional/Advanced Paper: The Card and Krueger minimum-wage study as a classic quasi-experiment](https://davidcard.berkeley.edu/papers/njmin-aer.pdf)
- [Optional/Advanced Article: Technical Nobel background on the economics of natural experiments](https://www.nobelprize.org/uploads/2021/10/advanced-economicsciencesprize2021.pdf)

### AI Assignment

Complete the Week 7 module on the course AI tutor site by **Sunday, May 10 at 11:59pm**.

---

## Week 8 (May 12–14): Risk, Insurance & Labor

### Class Prep: Risk and Insurance

- [E4E Video: Adverse selection and why insurance markets can unravel](https://econ4everyone.uchicago.edu/video/adverse-selection/)
- [Podcast: Why insurance exists and how it helps people manage risk](https://www.mercatus.org/marginal-revolution-podcast/economics-insurance)
- [Podcast: Why insurance markets matter, and why insurance is more interesting than it sounds](https://freakonomics.com/podcast/insurance-is-sexy-discuss/)

### Class Prep: Labor Markets

- [Podcast transcript: How supply, demand, and matching work in labor markets](https://www.npr.org/2022/08/10/1116835727/summer-school-5-car-parts-celery-the-labor-market)
- [Podcast transcript: How geography and inequality shape access to jobs in different labor markets](https://www.npr.org/sections/money/2014/08/22/342232976/episode-562-a-mall-divided)
- [Podcast transcript: How taxes affect work incentives and labor-market decisions](https://www.npr.org/sections/money/2013/04/12/177063399/episode-451-why-some-people-love-tax-day)

### Optional Readings

None this week.

### AI Assignment

Complete the Week 8 module on the course AI tutor site by **Sunday, May 17 at 11:59pm**.

---

## Week 9 (May 19–21): Technology, AI & Midterm

**This Week**

- Tuesday (May 19): Complete the materials below before class.
- Thursday (May 21): **Midterm in class**

### Class Prep: Technology, Growth, and AI

- [E4E Video: Productivity growth and why technology raises living standards over time](https://econ4everyone.uchicago.edu/video/productivity/)
- [Article: How restaurant automation is changing work in the service sector](https://www.npr.org/2026/03/13/nx-s1-5733110/automation-chef-wok-restaurant-industry)

### Optional Readings

None this week.

### AI Assignment

*No AI tutor assignment this week.*
